_comment: RAT cross-axis COMPOSITION golden data — the ADR-003 'run against each other on golden data' gate (reviews/07 Part C). Unlike the per-axis <axis>-v1.json vectors (which test ONE axis in isolation), this drives a full data-plane PIPELINE — catalog (resolve refs) -> engine (SQL transform) -> format (write), wired by CAPABILITY through a mediating gateway, with the bulk Arrow moving over REAL Arrow Flight between axes. The pipeline is run for the four ADR-003 cross-combinations (baseline + one-axis substitution for format, catalog, engine; storage held at A) and every combination MUST produce this identical `expected_target`. That is what proves the contracts COMPOSE, not just that each axis conforms alone. See examples/composition/README.md.
transform_sql: SELECT region, CAST(SUM(amount) AS BIGINT) AS total FROM orders GROUP BY region ORDER BY region
sql_note: CAST(... AS BIGINT) is deliberate: DuckDB's SUM(int) yields a 128-bit decimal/hugeint while DataFusion's yields int64 — a real cross-engine Arrow-dialect divergence (ADR-003's exact target). The CAST pins the output type so any conformant engine produces an identical int64 column; without it, an engine-substitution would change the result schema. This is a cross-axis assumption surfaced by composition, not by per-axis conformance.
